items: Effect-layer clips (2 tabs) show tabs stretched evenly across full panel width.
All other clip types (3-tab audio, 4-tab visual/text) render tabs correctly with even spacing.
Dragging any external item (file, text tool, effect layer, asset) above the first track shows a top drop indicator with correct label.
Dragging an existing clip above the first track shows the top drop indicator.
Dropping on the top indicator creates a new track at index 0 and shifts existing tracks down — for both external and internal drops.
The bottom drop zone continues to work identically (no regression).
The top indicator is absolutely positioned and does not shift track row layout during drag.
Hit-testing remains accurate: hovering over the first track row still targets that track, not the top drop zone.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline indicator typing: Step 3 fixes `isNewTrackTop`, but it still leaves `DropIndicator.tsx` reading `position.trackId` without declaring or forwarding that field.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `DropIndicatorPosition` currently ends at `newTrackKind`/`reject` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:23-27`, but `DropIndicator` reads `position?.trackId` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:68-77`. Upstream, `toIndicatorPosition()` still only forwards `isNewTrack`, `newTrackKind`, and `reject` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts:64-80`. The revised plan only adds `isNewTrackTop`, so the strict-TS mismatch around `trackId` remains unplanned.

unresolved_flags.concern: Workspace hygiene: Step 2 still uses `git checkout HEAD -- <file>` against dirty files even though the repo still has unstaged edits in those paths and the plan itself keeps an open question about why the revert exists.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Current repo state still shows unstaged modifications in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx` (`git status --short`). The revised plan's Step 2 would overwrite six of those files before resolving whether the revert was intentional.

rationale: The remaining two flags are executor-level details, not planning gaps. The trackId typing issue will surface as a TS error during execution and is trivially fixable inline. The workspace-restore concern is resolved by context: the user explicitly requested the top drop zone feature, the uncommitted changes revert it, and discarding those reverts is the intended behavior — not accidental data loss. The plan now covers all drop paths (external + internal), fixes the plumbing bug, and addresses layout shift. Ready to execute.
signals_assessment: Weighted score dropped sharply from 6.0 to 1.25 across iterations, with all 3 original significant flags resolved. Plan delta of 78% reflects substantial revision that addressed the prior gate's concerns (existing-clip path, indicator plumbing, layout shift). No recurring critiques. Two new flags raised at significant severity but both are execution-time concerns rather than architectural gaps. Preflight remains clean.
warnings: When restoring files in Step 2, the executor should briefly diff the unstaged changes against HEAD to confirm they are indeed pure reverts before discarding.
The executor must add `trackId` to `DropIndicatorPosition` and forward it in `toIndicatorPosition()` alongside `isNewTrackTop` — the plan's Step 3 is incomplete on this point.

settled_decisions.decision: Phase 1 tab fix (grid-cols-2 and grid-cols-5 in TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS) is correct and independent.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 1, no flags have challenged it.

settled_decisions.decision: Restore committed HEAD files as baseline, then fix forward.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 1. The committed code has working detection/insertion logic; only plumbing and positioning need fixes.

settled_decisions.decision: Top indicator must be absolutely positioned (not normal flow) to avoid layout shift.
settled_decisions.rationale: Resolved the layout-shift flag in iteration 2. Absolute positioning with pointer-events-none is the correct approach.

settled_decisions.decision: Internal clip drag support via addTrack's existing index parameter.
settled_decisions.rationale: Resolved the existing-clip flag in iteration 2. addTrack already accepts an index param, so wiring it through createTrackAndMoveClip is minimal and correct.

settled_decisions.decision: Executor must also add trackId to DropIndicatorPosition and forward it — treat this as part of Step 3, not a separate concern.
settled_decisions.rationale: Gate warning from accepted tradeoff. Review should verify this was done, not re-litigate whether it's needed.

orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Two UX improvements: (1) Drop-to-create-track at the TOP of the timeline, not just the bottom. When dragging an item (external file, text tool, effect layer, or existing clip) near the top edge of the timeline, show a temporary drop zone above the first track. Dropping there creates a new track at position 0 and places the item on it. The existing tracks shift down to make room. This mirrors the existing bottom drop zone behavior. (2) Effect layer tab layout: the ClipPanel tabs (effects, timing, position, audio, text) should be evenly spaced across the full width regardless of how many are visible. Currently when only 2 tabs show (like for effect-layer clips), they're shoved to the left. They should stretch to fill the available width.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline indicator typing: Step 3 fixes `isNewTrackTop`, but it still leaves `DropIndicator.tsx` reading `position.trackId` without declaring or forwarding that field.

Workspace hygiene: Step 2 still uses `git checkout HEAD -- <file>` against dirty files even though the repo still has unstaged edits in those paths and the plan itself keeps an open question about why the revert exists.

resolved_flags.concern: Timeline top-drop: the plan does not implement the user's existing-clip case, so dragging an existing clip to the top edge would still create the new track at the bottom.
resolved_flags.resolution: Addressed all 3 significant flags: (1) Added Step 5 covering internal clip drag path through `useClipDrag.ts` → `createTrackAndMoveClip()` → `addTrack()`, leveraging `addTrack`'s existing `index` parameter. (2) Added Step 3 fixing the broken plumbing — `isNewTrackTop` must be added to `DropIndicatorPosition` interface and forwarded in `toIndicatorPosition()`. (3) Added Step 4 changing the top indicator from normal-flow to absolute positioning to avoid displacing track rows and breaking coordinate math.

resolved_flags.concern: Timeline indicator plumbing: Step 3 understates the problem; restoring `TimelineCanvas` alone is not enough because the indicator path currently drops the top-vs-bottom state before it reaches the UI.
resolved_flags.resolution: Addressed all 3 significant flags: (1) Added Step 5 covering internal clip drag path through `useClipDrag.ts` → `createTrackAndMoveClip()` → `addTrack()`, leveraging `addTrack`'s existing `index` parameter. (2) Added Step 3 fixing the broken plumbing — `isNewTrackTop` must be added to `DropIndicatorPosition` interface and forwarded in `toIndicatorPosition()`. (3) Added Step 4 changing the top indicator from normal-flow to absolute positioning to avoid displacing track rows and breaking coordinate math.

resolved_flags.concern: Timeline top-drop layout: the proposed top drop zone rendering is likely to destabilize hit-testing because it inserts new layout above the rows without adjusting row coordinate math.
resolved_flags.resolution: Addressed all 3 significant flags: (1) Added Step 5 covering internal clip drag path through `useClipDrag.ts` → `createTrackAndMoveClip()` → `addTrack()`, leveraging `addTrack`'s existing `index` parameter. (2) Added Step 3 fixing the broken plumbing — `isNewTrackTop` must be added to `DropIndicatorPosition` interface and forwarded in `toIndicatorPosition()`. (3) Added Step 4 changing the top indicator from normal-flow to absolute positioning to avoid displacing track rows and breaking coordinate math.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 6.0 -> 1.25. Plan deltas: 78.1%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 3. Open significant flags: 2.
